figures.caption: Fig. 3. Qualitative results of our Noise Removal module. White circles in the middle column show the artifacts.
Fig. 4. Qualitative results of 3D scene editing tasks on scenes, showing sharp object boundaries and high visual consistency.

Fig. 5. Comparison of object removal quality where FlashSplat produces large artifacts and blurry boundaries, whereas our method achieves significantly cleaner and sharper object boundaries. [Top : bear scene, Bottom: horns scene]
Fig. 6. Effect of varying γp which controls the contribution of prior learned labels during label reassignment for scene bear.

Fig. 1. Overview of our robust 3D-GS segmentation framework. (A) Multiview mask generation with SAM-HQ for precise extraction and DEVA for consistent labelling via bidirectional temporal propagation. (B) Training: 16 dimensional per-Gaussian object features optimized jointly, projected via ϕ(f) to 256 class probabilities, supervised by true labels for label-aware rendering. (C) Inference for label reassignment: From user selected point (x, y, z), query K-nearest Gaussian neighbours, project Gaussians to masks for majority voting with priors to form contribution matrix R256×N , and extract binary mask for object k.
